title: Scheduling, Affinity, Taints, Topology, Priority, Preemption, and Eviction · Kubernetes Operations Room
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working placement and disruption decision record for scheduling, affinity, taints, topology, priority, preemption, and eviction. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of placement and disruption decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use trusted node labels, nodeSelector, required and preferred node affinity, operators, weights, and ignored-during-execution semantics.
Design inter-Pod affinity and anti-affinity with selectors, namespaces, topology keys, scheduler cost, and failure-domain consequences.
Trace NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, and NoExecute taints, matching tolerations, toleration time, node conditions, and admission-added behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Node Selector Affinity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 3. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Node Selector Affinity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use trusted node labels, nodeselector, required and preferred node affinity, operators, weights, and ignored-during-execution semantics. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Node Selector Affinity, keep K8s Node Selector Affinity connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Priority Preemption Eviction
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction-1lsgym". Tailor its placement-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Priority Preemption Eviction.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Priority Preemption Eviction into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, keep K8s Priority Preemption Eviction connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction-1lsgym
  - competency: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction
    artifact: placement-policy.yaml
    decision: priority preemption eviction
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: 1lsgym
    change: |
      node-affinity: trusted-workload-class
      toleration: dedicated=intake:NoSchedule
      topologySpread: zones-maxSkew-1
      priorityClass: intake-critical
      preemption-policy: evidence-gated
      eviction: graceful-and-budgeted
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o wide && kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=status-api
    failure: Reject toleration-as-attraction, preferred-as-required, replica count as spread proof, or priority as permission for unbounded disruption.
    repair: Derive eligible nodes and domains, calculate skew, inspect unschedulable reasons, separate preemption from eviction, and preserve graceful recovery.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Priority Preemption Eviction structural check is only one gate. placement and disruption decision record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s Priority Preemption Eviction
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 6. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName. Competency probe: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s Priority Preemption Eviction.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, poddisruptionbudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, api eviction, graceful termination, and fairness. For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, keep K8s Priority Preemption Eviction connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

stimulus.title: K8s Priority Preemption Eviction failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof. This does not satisfy K8s Priority Preemption Eviction for placement and disruption decision record.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s Priority Preemption Eviction condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible K8s Priority Preemption Eviction output and hide the violated placement and disruption decision record assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for K8s Priority Preemption Eviction.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.
